core_mechanism: A self-improving reverse-Hölder (Gehring-type) higher-integrability estimate for the gradient of a solution to a linear boundary-value problem — obtained by controlling a nonlinearly reweighted gradient (the gradient scaled by a power of itself) via an a priori global second-order bound — which bootstraps and enlarges the range of exponents p for which the problem is solvable.
brief_reason: The abstract's method introduces a power-weighted nonlinear gradient quantity in place of the linear gradient to obtain a global second-order estimate, which in turn yields an improved reverse Hölder inequality that extends the known range of L^p solvability.
